title: [LV] Allow tail folding with IVs with outside users
author: lukel97
description:
#149042 added last-active-lane and removed the restriction that we couldn't tail fold loops that had outside users (in AllowedExit).

However we still have a restriction that IVs can't have outside users. This was added separately to the AllowedExit restriction in #81609, but it looks like #149042 didn't remove it.

AFAICT we currently extract the correct lane for IVs, so this PR relaxes the restriction. This helps a good few loops get tail folded in llvm-test-suite.

-force-tail-folding-style=none was added to pr5881-scev-expansion.ll to preserve the original scev expansion, since otherwise we end up with a cttz.elts(false, false, true, true) that blocks SCEV analysis. We should probably teach ConstantFolding to fold it.

tests.test_body:
define i32 @test(ptr %arr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp1 = icmp ugt i64 %n, 1
  br i1 %cmp1, label %preheader, label %done

preheader:                                        ; preds = %entry
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %preheader
  %conv = phi i64 [ %conv2, %loop ], [ 1, %preheader ]
  %i = phi i8 [ %inc, %loop ], [ 1, %preheader ]
  %sub = add nsw i64 %conv, -1
  %ptr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %arr, i64 %sub
  store i32 65, ptr %ptr, align 4
  %inc = add i8 %i, 1
  %conv2 = zext i8 %inc to i64
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i64 %conv2, %n
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop, label %load_val, !llvm.loop !0

load_val:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %final = phi i64 [ %conv, %loop ]
  %ptr2 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %arr, i64 %final
  %val = load i32, ptr %ptr2, align 4
  br label %done

done:                                             ; preds = %load_val, %entry
  %value = phi i32 [ %val, %load_val ], [ 0, %entry ]
  ret i32 %value
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1, !2, !3}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.disable"}
!2 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.predicate.enable", i1 true}
!3 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i1 true}

define void @iv_start_from_shl_of_previous_iv(ptr %dst) {
entry:
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %entry
  %iv.1 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop.1 ]
  %gep.1 = getelementptr i8, ptr %dst, i64 %iv.1
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.1, align 1
  %iv.1.next = add i64 %iv.1, 1
  %cmp.1 = icmp eq i64 %iv.1, 0
  br i1 %cmp.1, label %loop.1, label %loop.1.exit

loop.1.exit:                                      ; preds = %loop.1
  %iv.1.shl = shl i64 %iv.1, 1
  br label %loop.2

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1.exit
  %iv.2 = phi i64 [ %iv.1.shl, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %dst, i64 %iv.2
  store i8 1, ptr %gep.2, align 1
  %iv.2.next = add i64 %iv.2, 1
  %cmp.2 = icmp eq i64 %iv.2, 100
  br i1 %cmp.2, label %exit, label %loop.2

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret void
}

define void @test1_pr58811(ptr %dst) {
entry:
  br label %loop.1.preheader

loop.1.preheader:                                 ; preds = %unreachable.bb, %entry
  %iv.1.ph = phi i32 [ %sub93.2, %unreachable.bb ], [ 0, %entry ]
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %loop.1.preheader
  %iv.1 = phi i32 [ %iv.1.next, %loop.1 ], [ %iv.1.ph, %loop.1.preheader ]
  %iv.2 = phi i32 [ %iv.2.next, %loop.1 ], [ 0, %loop.1.preheader ]
  %iv.2.next = add i32 %iv.2, 1
  %iv.1.next = add i32 %iv.2, %iv.1
  br i1 false, label %loop.1, label %loop.2.preheader

loop.2.preheader:                                 ; preds = %loop.1
  %iv.1.lcssa = phi i32 [ %iv.1, %loop.1 ]
  br label %loop.2

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.2.preheader
  %iv.3 = phi i16 [ %iv.3.next, %loop.2 ], [ 0, %loop.2.preheader ]
  %iv.4 = phi i32 [ %iv.4.next, %loop.2 ], [ 0, %loop.2.preheader ]
  %gep.dst = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %dst, i16 %iv.3
  store i32 %iv.4, ptr %gep.dst, align 4
  %iv.4.next = sub i32 %iv.4, %iv.1.lcssa
  %iv.3.next = add i16 %iv.3, 1
  %cmp88.1 = icmp ult i16 %iv.3, 198
  br i1 %cmp88.1, label %loop.2, label %loop.3.preheader

loop.3.preheader:                                 ; preds = %loop.2
  %iv.4.lcssa = phi i32 [ %iv.4, %loop.2 ]
  br label %loop.3

loop.3:                                           ; preds = %loop.3, %loop.3.preheader
  %iv.5 = phi i32 [ %sub93.2, %loop.3 ], [ 0, %loop.3.preheader ]
  %sub93.2 = sub i32 %iv.5, %iv.4.lcssa
  br label %loop.3

unreachable.bb:                                   ; No predecessors!
  br label %loop.1.preheader
}

define i32 @f(ptr noalias %p, i32 %start, i32 %step, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv2 = phi i32 [ %start, %entry ], [ %iv2.next, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add i32 %iv, 1
  %iv2.next = add i32 %iv2, %step
  %ec = icmp eq i32 %iv, %n
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %iv2
}
